description: Molecule: collapsible disclosure list composed of atoms (buttons, text) and structured as a compound component: Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent.

type.description: Controls whether multiple items can be expanded at once.

collapsible.description: When true in single mode, the open item can be collapsed so that no items are expanded.

defaultValue.description: Initial open item values. In single mode, only the first value is used.

value.description: Controlled open item values. In single mode, at most one value is respected.

disabled.description: Disables all interaction with the accordion.

onValueChange.description: Called when the set of open item values changes.

orientation.description: Keyboard navigation orientation. Vertical accordions use Up/Down, horizontal use Left/Right.

variants.description: One item open at a time, one item must always remain open.
One item open at a time, all items may be collapsed.
Multiple items can be expanded at once.

keyboardShortcuts.action: Move focus into or out of the accordion following document order.
Move focus to the next AccordionTrigger in vertical orientation; wrap to first when at the end.
Move focus to the previous AccordionTrigger in vertical orientation; wrap to last when at the beginning.
Move focus to the next AccordionTrigger in horizontal orientation; wrap to first when at the end.
Move focus to the previous AccordionTrigger in horizontal orientation; wrap to last when at the beginning.
Move focus to the first AccordionTrigger.
Move focus to the last AccordionTrigger.
Toggle the expanded state of the focused AccordionItem, following the single/multiple and collapsible rules. Ignore when the item is disabled.
Toggle the expanded state of the focused AccordionItem, following the single/multiple and collapsible rules. Ignore when the item is disabled.

root.description: Container for the accordion items. Renders the Accordion root component.

item.description: Container for a single accordion entry. Wraps a trigger and its associated content.

trigger.description: Interactive element that toggles its associated content. Typically rendered as a button atom.

content.description: Region that shows or hides detailed content for an item.

usage.summary: Use Accordion to show and hide sections of related content. It is a molecule composed of atoms and exposed as a compound component with Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, and AccordionContent.
do: Use Accordion for grouped, optionally dense content such as FAQs, filter groups, or settings sections.
Prefer single mode when users should focus on one section at a time.
Use multiple mode when users benefit from scanning several sections simultaneously.
dont: Don't use Accordion for primary navigation — prefer navigation components instead.
Don't nest interactive controls inside triggers; keep triggers as simple button-like atoms.
Don't rely solely on animation to convey expanded state; keep aria-expanded in sync.

platformNotes.web: Implement using ARIA accordion guidance: triggers as buttons with aria-expanded and aria-controls pointing at region content.
platformNotes.ios: Map Accordion to a vertical stack of disclosure groups. Use SwiftUI DisclosureGroup or custom Button + content pairs.
platformNotes.android: Map Accordion to a column of expandable items, e.g., Material3 ExposedDropdownMenuBox or ExpansionPanels.

changes: Initial draft of accordion molecule contract with compound composition, ARIA keyboard pattern, and state-machine oriented props.
